sourceMovie.title: Migration
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Migration works as a family movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The family and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Migration's family stayed with you, Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Migration excels in family, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Storks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Storks (2016) is the natural next step after Migration: same family DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Migration who respond to its family will find Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: A Grand Day Out
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Migration fans keep landing on A Grand Day Out (1990) — the family is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp draws from the same family well as Migration, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1990 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Wild Thornberrys Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wild Thornberrys Movie (2002) captures the family appeal of Migration without simply imitating it. The comedy streak running through both films is what puts The Wild Thornberrys Movie at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Simpsons Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The family appeal of Migration — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into The Simpsons Movie (2007). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Chicken Run
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Chicken Run (2000) clearly understood what makes family work — the same quality that keeps Migration rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Epic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Epic (2013) is the kind of film Migration fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The family overlap is real, but it's the uplifting emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Migration?
faq.answer: Try Fantastic Mr. Fox, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit, Storks—each shares DNA with Migration while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Migration?
faq.answer: Migration (2023) is best described as Family, Comedy, Adventure, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Migration worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
